value.reason: The cited page documents OpenAI Codex "Automations" — a scheduled/triggered runs feature, not background/async execution as a distinct capability. The page explicitly describes standalone automations that "start fresh runs on a schedule" and thread automations that run "on a schedule" or at intervals — this maps directly to the ADJACENT capability "Scheduled / triggered runs," not to the target capability of background/async execution (i.e., running tasks asynchronously without blocking). While the page mentions that automations "run in the background," that phrasing describes the execution mode of scheduled automations, not a standalone async/background execution capability. The cell label "Scheduled Tasks" and description ("run on a schedule independently of any conversation thread") further confirm this is describing the adjacent scheduled-runs capability rather than the target background/async execution capability. The page does not document a general-purpose async or non-blocking task execution API or feature distinct from scheduling.
